Metadata.title: DouzePoints - Eurovision Scoreboard Simulator
Metadata.description: Experience the excitement of Eurovision with DouzePoints, the interactive scoreboard simulator. Vote for countries, watch points accumulate, and relive the magic of Eurovision Song Contest

error.title: Oops! Something Went Wrong
error.errorInfoReloadPage: First, try clicking "Reload Page" to reload the page keeping all local data.
error.errorInfoResetApplication: If the problem persists, click "Reset Application" to clear all local data and reload the app.
error.errorInfoNotAffected: Your saved profile data and cloud-synced information won't be affected.
error.errorInfoReport: The error is automatically reported to the developer. But you can still send an email to the developer with the additional error details (e.g., what you did to trigger the error) at

error.pleaseSelectValidImageFile: Please select a valid image file.

validation.qualifiersCannotMoveToPrecedentStage: Qualifiers cannot move to a precedent stage ({stageName} -> {targetStageName}).

eventSetupModal.confirmNotGFOnlyDescription: This will reset your setup and restore all shows (Semi-Finals + Grand Final).

In the next step you can override the points system for this stage on the General tab. If you continue without changing it, the simulation may not work correctly while there are fewer participants than the number of unique points system items plus one ({uniqueCount} + 1).

feedbackInfo.bugReportDetailsItem4: and any helpful details like screenshots or videos
feedbackInfo.datesAreApproximate: The dates are approximate and may change
feedbackInfo.translationIssueDescription: If you noticed some translation issues, please let me know by sending an email to
feedbackInfo.donationCardIntro: Hi, I'm Oleksandr — a Eurovision fan and developer from Ukraine. DouzePoints is a free project used by thousands of Eurovision fans around the world and is built and maintained entirely by me.
feedbackInfo.donationCardSupport: If you'd like to support future development and help me continue improving the platform, your contribution would mean a lot to me.

gettingStarted.title: Quick Start Guide
gettingStarted.content: <list><item>Select a year from the top-left dropdown to load the official Eurovision lineup, then press Start to launch the simulation.</item><item>During voting, click any country on the scoreboard to award it the current points, or use 'Vote randomly' to let the app decide.</item><item>Progress through each stage by awarding all votes and clicking Continue</item><item>After all stages are done, view the detailed stats or generate a shareable scoreboard image.</item></list>

signIn.title: Unlocking More Features with an Account
signIn.content: <list><item>Click the profile icon in the widgets section to sign in with Google.</item><item>Signing in unlocks cloud saving for your contests, creating and publishing custom themes, adding custom entries, and social features.</item><item>Without an account you can still browse and apply public themes and contests, but you cannot save, copy, like, or create your own.</item><item>Your existing local data is preserved when you sign in.</item></list>

savingContests.title: Saving & Loading Contests
savingContests.content: Click the save icon on the right side of the current contest card or open the Contests widget and click 'Create Contest' to save your current setup and simulation results to the cloud. You can reload any saved contest later, reset it to the last saved version, or share it with anyone via a link. Public contests can be browsed and loaded by other users.

customEntries.title: Custom Entries
customEntries.content: Signed-in users can create fictional or non-standard entries — for example, a country that never participated in Eurovision, or a made-up one — with a custom name and flag (upload or URL). Custom entries can be organized into groups and imported from other users' contests.

yearData.title: Year Selection & Official Data
yearData.content: Use the top-left dropdown to pick an ESC edition (2004–2026) or a JESC edition (2016–2025). Selecting a year automatically loads the official lineup for that edition. The same dropdown also lets you quickly load one of your saved contests.

countryAssignment.title: Country Assignment
countryAssignment.content: The countries shown when you pick a year are a starting point, not a locked list. In the Setup modal, click any country to move it between stages (Semi-Final 1, Semi-Final 2, Grand Final) or mark it as Not Participating. You can also add countries from other years, other continents, or your own custom entries.

grandFinalOnly.title: Grand Final Only Mode
grandFinalOnly.content: Toggle the 'GF only' switch in the setup to skip the Semi-Finals and run a standalone Grand Final, with all entries placed directly into it. Toggling it back restores the full three-show structure (Semi-Final 1, Semi-Final 2, Grand Final).

customStages.title: Custom Stages
customStages.content: Beyond the standard Semi-Finals and Grand Final, you can create any number of additional stages — Heats, Quarter-Finals, and more. Each stage has its own name, voting mode (Jury + Televote, Televote-only, or Combined), and qualification rules specifying how many entries advance and to which stage. Advanced: rank-based qualification lets you send different rank ranges to different follow-up stages.

votingModes.title: Voting Mode per Stage
votingModes.content: Each stage can use a different voting system, configured in the stage editor's Settings tab:Jury and Televote — two separate rounds: first all jury votes are given out country by country, then all televote points are revealed togetherTelevote only — skip jury voting; only televote points are awardedJury only — skip televote; only jury points are awardedCombined — jury and televote points are awarded in a single round per voting country (currently it may work inaccurately)The default for the Grand Final is Jury and Televote, matching the standard Eurovision format.

votingCountries.title: Voting Countries
votingCountries.content: In each stage's 'Voters' tab, choose exactly which countries cast votes during that stage. You can include or exclude any participating country and optionally add 'Rest of the World' as an additional voter. Voter sets are configured per stage, so Semi-Finals and the Grand Final can have completely different voters. If you want to use the original voting countries from the year, you click on the 'Load year data' button.

runningOrder.title: Running Order
runningOrder.content: Before starting a stage, open the Running Order tab to drag and drop entries into your preferred performance order. The running order is also used as a tiebreaker: if two entries have identical total points and televote points, the one that performed earlier ranks higher.

customPointsSystem.title: Custom Points System
customPointsSystem.content: Go to 'Settings → Voting' to configure the points awarded at each position. You can add, remove, or reorder values. Enable 'Separate jury / televote points system' to use a completely different scale for jury votes and televote. Click on the sparkles icon to toggle the Douze Points animation for a specific point value.

predefinedVoting.title: Predefined Voting & Presets
predefinedVoting.content: Enable 'Predefined voting' in 'Settings → Voting' to fill in every country's votes before the simulation starts. A full voting matrix lets you assign specific points from each voter to each entry. Save the configuration locally as a named preset to reload it anytime — perfect for replaying official Eurovision results or sharing a specific scenario.

oddsRandomness.title: Odds & Randomness
oddsRandomness.content: In 'Settings → Odds', assign jury and televote odds (0–100) to each country to influence how random votes are distributed. Higher odds mean a better chance of receiving more points. The Randomness Level slider controls how closely the simulation follows those odds: slide left for predictable results that mirror the odds, slide right for a chaotic, anything-can-happen simulation.

presentationMode.title: Presentation Mode
presentationMode.content: Enable 'Presentation mode' in 'Settings → Voting' to add an auto-play panel below the scoreboard. Set the speed (0.5–7.5 seconds per action) and press Play to let the simulation run hands-free — ideal for streaming or recording. Enable 'Auto start presentation' to have it begin automatically as soon as voting starts.

pickQualifiers.title: Pick Qualifiers Without Awarding Points
pickQualifiers.content: Enable 'Pick qualifiers without awarding points' in 'Settings → Voting' to select which entries advance to the next stage without running the full voting process. During a Semi-Final, click any entry on the board to qualify it directly, or press 'Choose randomly'. This is ideal when you only care about who advances, not the detailed scores. Note: the stage's voting mode still affects how random qualifiers are selected.

moreOptions.title: More Voting Options
moreOptions.content: Additional options in Settings → Voting and the simulation header:Split screen qualifier reveal — an ESC 2025-style moment where several candidates appear side-by-side and you click one to qualify them. Configure 2–6 candidates per reveal, and whether the last qualifier also uses the split screen.Televote reveal order — set to lowest-to-highest to announce televote results from fewest to most points, as in ESC 2016–2018.Allow points to the same entry multiple times — enables a voting country to award multiple point tokens to the same entry. This simulates the pre-1975 Eurovision voting system where each jury distributed ten equal tokens freely.Group random jury voting — the 'Vote randomly' button awards points in realistic batches (1–8+10 together, or just 12) instead of one value at a time.Undo and Restart — Undo steps back up to 100 actions; Restart resets the full simulation.

yearThemes.title: Year Themes & Auto-Sync
yearThemes.content: Many ESC and JESC editions have a built-in theme inspired by that year's visual identity — colors, fonts, flag styles, and animations. Selecting a year from the dropdown automatically suggests the matching theme. If you switch the year after a theme is already active, a Sync button appears in the header to apply the correct year theme.

browseThemes.title: Browsing Community Themes
browseThemes.content: Open the Themes widget to browse public themes created by other users — no account needed to browse or apply them. Sign in to like a theme, save it to your collection for quick access, copy it as a starting point for your own, or create one from scratch.

customThemeBuilder.title: Custom Theme Builder
customThemeBuilder.content: Signed-in users can build a fully custom theme: choose a primary color, upload a background image, select a font, and override the colors for every scoreboard state independently (jury active, televote unfinished/active/finished, unqualified entries, and rank badges). Flag shapes, points container shapes, and board animation styles are all configurable. A live preview panel shows exactly how each change will look on a real country item.

soundEffects.title: Sound Effects & Background Audio
soundEffects.content: Inside the theme builder's Sound Effects section, attach audio clips to specific moments: the 12-point award, stage start, jury reveal, televote reveal, winner announcement, and a looped background track. Paste an https:// URL or upload a file directly (max 7 MB per file). Each sound supports an optional delay in seconds.

visualDetails.title: Visual Details & Animations
visualDetails.content: The 'Visual Details' section of the theme builder lets you fine-tune animations and layout: board movement style (classic Flip or the ESC 2025 Teleport), the douze points animation (Parallelograms from ESC 2023 or Hearts Grid from ESC 2025), flag shape (large or small rectangle, square, circle, circle with border, or hidden), points container shape, uppercase entry names, and more.

finalStats.title: Final Stats & Voting Breakdown
finalStats.content: After any stage finishes, tap 'Stats' in the phase actions bar to open the detailed results. The Breakdown tab shows a full voting matrix — every voter's exact point allocations to every entry, split into Jury, Televote, and Total columns. Use it to see exactly who gave (or didn't give) points to any entry.

splitStats.title: Jury vs. Televote Split
splitStats.content: The Split tab in the stats modal places each entry's jury ranking next to its televote ranking side by side, making it easy to spot the biggest discrepancies between expert opinion and the public vote. Entries with a large gap between the two are where the drama happens.

shareScoreboard.title: Share Scoreboard as Image
shareScoreboard.content: Hit 'Share' in the simulation header to open the image generator. Customise the layout (1–4 columns), aspect ratio (Landscape 16:9, Square 1:1, or Portrait 3:4), title, subtitle, and whether to show rankings and points. Download the result as a PNG or use native sharing on mobile.

shareStats.title: Share Stats as Image
shareStats.content: Inside the Stats modal, a share button lets you export the full results table (breakdown, split, or summary) as a downloadable PNG. Great for sharing detailed scoreboard breakdowns on social media or in Eurovision fan communities.

leaderboard.title: Global Country Leaderboard
leaderboard.content: The country leaderboard (accessible from the 'Contests → Public Contests') aggregates results across all completed public contests. It tracks wins, top-3/5/10 finishes, average Grand Final points, and qualifying streaks. Use the year filter to narrow it down to a specific edition or range, or toggle 'Only Eurovision countries' to exclude non-standard entries.

myStats.title: My Entry Leaderboard & Stats
myStats.content: The 'My entry leaderboard' in the 'Contests → Your Contests' widget shows personal stats from all your completed contests: wins, nul points, best result, and current qualifying streak for every country and custom entry you've used. Click any row to see a full contest-by-contest breakdown with placements and points.

communityFollowing.title: Following Other Users
communityFollowing.content: On any user's public profile (accessible by clicking their username on a public theme or contest), you can follow them. Followed users' new themes and contests appear in a combined 'Following' feed on your own profile page. Without an account you can still browse the Public tabs in the Themes and Contests widgets.

uiPreferences.title: UI Preferences
uiPreferences.content: Customize the look and feel in Settings → UI Preferences:Flag styles — switch to heart icons, minimalistic round/square flags, or keep default rectangular onesAlways show rankings — keep position numbers visible at all times, plus a rank-change indicator showing movement up or downPopups — toggle the qualifiers popup, winner announcement, and confetti independentlyFullscreen mode — enter fullscreen for a fully immersive scoreboard viewJury voting progress bar — shows how many countries have voted so far in the current jury roundCustom background image — upload your own image to replace the theme's backgroundOverride theme font — lock in a specific font regardless of which theme is activeAudio — set separate volume levels for sound effects and background music, or disable all theme audio at onceWinter effects — enable falling snow with an adjustable intensity sliderEnable winner reveal — the last televote is revealed with special animation (ESC 2026 style)

confirmations.title: Confirmation Popups
confirmations.content: In Settings → Confirmations you can toggle individual confirmation dialogs on or off — for example, the prompts for cancelling a simulation, deleting a contest, or logging out. Disable the ones you find disruptive; they can be re-enabled at any time. 'Reset All Confirmations' restores every disabled prompt at once.

language.title: Language Support
language.content: DouzePoints supports 8 languages: English, French,Spanish, German, Italian, Greek, Polish, and Ukrainian. Change the active language in 'Settings → UI Preferences → Language'. All languages other than English are currently produced by machine translation, so occasional inaccuracies may appear.

feedback.title: Feedback & What's New
feedback.content: Found a bug, have a feature idea, or just want to leave a comment? Click the info icon in the header (next to this guide button) to open the Feedback panel. It also shows a full changelog ('What's New') and a roadmap of upcoming features. You can file a report on GitHub Issues or send an email directly to the developer.

relations.title: Diaspora & affinity
relations.subtitle: Blocs, neighbours & rivalries nudge simulated votes beyond song quality.
relations.blocsSub: Regional voting blocs — expand any to tune its member pairs.
relations.specialSub: The most influential neighbour, diaspora & rivalry ties beyond the blocs.
relations.broadSub: The full data set from 20+ years of results. Max realism, advanced.
relations.broadOnNote: All historical pairs is on — it already includes every bloc, so turning a bloc off won't remove those relations. Disable it to control blocs individually.
relations.broadBrowseHint: This set isn't applied. Browse and tune any pair — your edits are saved to Your pairs and apply on their own. Turn it on to apply the whole set.
relations.pickTwo: Pick two countries to preview the relationship
relations.overrideHint: Adding a pair that already exists overrides the preset. Set it to 0 to cancel an inherited bias entirely.
relations.noPairs: No custom pairs yet. Edit a bloc pair or add one above.

odds.goBackToSetupAndSelectCountriesForEventT: Go back to the setup and select countries for the event to set their odds
odds.oddsTooltip:
Each country has separate jury and televote odds (0–100).

Higher number = better chance of getting more points.

Initial values are based on countries' real Eurovision results for the selected year.
odds.randomnessLevelTooltip:
The Randomness Level slider controls predictability:

Low = more realistic, following odds closely.

High = more chaotic, unpredictable.
odds.pointsSpreadTooltip:
The Points Spread slider controls the gap between winners and losers:

Low = tighter scores, smaller point differences.

High = a bigger gap between the top and bottom.

voting.allowMultiplePointsToSameEntry: Allow points to the same entry multiple times
voting.allowMultiplePointsToSameEntryTooltip: When enabled, a voting country can award multiple point tokens to the same entry. This simulates the pre-1975 Eurovision voting system (used in 1957–1961, 1967–1970, and 1974), where each jury (and televote, if active) distributed ten equal tokens freely.
voting.sparklesIconTooltip: The icon indicates that the points will be animated when the country receives them. Click to toggle.
voting.pickQualifiersWithoutAwardingPointsToolt:
Click countries (or choose randomly) to qualify them without awarding points.

The Semi-Finals voting mode still affects random qualifiers and points distribution.
voting.enableSplitScreenQualifierRevealMode: Enable split screen qualifier reveal mode
voting.enableSplitScreenQualifierRevealModeTool: When enabled, several candidates will be shown on a split-screen, and you need to pick one qualifier (ESC 2025 format)
voting.splitScreenCandidatesCountTooltipDescrip: The number of candidates to show on the split screen. The default value is 3.
voting.enableSplitScreenForLastQualifier: Enable split screen for last qualifier
voting.enableSplitScreenForLastQualifierTooltip: When disabled, the last qualifier is picked from the remaining candidates without showing a split screen (ESC 2025 format)
voting.televoteRevealOrder: Televote reveal order: lowest to highest
voting.televoteRevealOrderTooltip: When enabled, the televote points will be announced from lowest to highest (ESC 2016–2018 system). Otherwise, order follows jury points.
voting.limitManualTelevotePointsTooltip: When enabled, limits the total televote points you can award (when manually entering points) and shows how many remain.
voting.groupRandomJuryVotingTooltip: When enabled, clicking 'Vote randomly' will award one set of jury points, grouped as either (1–8, 10) or (12).
voting.enablePredefinedVotingTooltip: When enabled, you can set each country's votes before the voting begins.
voting.enablePresentationModeTooltip: When enabled, displays the presentation panel, which allows you to run voting automatically, without requiring any clicks.
voting.autoStartPresentationTooltip: When enabled, the presentation will start automatically after the voting starts.

ui.dragAndDropToUpload: Drag and drop or click to upload
ui.warnBeforeManuallyAssigningTelevotePoint: Warn before manually assigning televote points
ui.imageSizeIsPrettyLarge: Image size is pretty large ({size}MB). This can slow down the simulation, so you might want to compress it or use a smaller image. Do you want to continue with this image?
ui.themeSoundVolumeHint: Applies to custom theme sound effects (not the simulation loop). Set to 0 to mute effects.
ui.audioPreferencesHint: The audio is available on custom themes only.
ui.themeAmbienceVolumeHint: Volume for the looped simulation background only.
ui.hideThemeSoundVolumeHudHint: You can still change volumes here in settings.
ui.disableAllThemeAudioHint: Mutes sound effects and simulation background from custom themes.
ui.themeSoundHudMuteToggle: Mute or restore effects and ambience

confirmations.description: Configure which confirmation popups are shown when you perform certain actions.
confirmations.resetAllSettingsDescription: Are you sure you want to reset all settings? This won't affect data saved to your account (profile, themes, contests, etc.).
confirmations.resetAllSettingsSuccess: All local settings have been reset successfully!
confirmations.changeContestDescription: A contest is in progress. Changing it will reset the current scoreboard and start a new setup.
confirmations.deleteCustomEntriesDescription: Are you sure you want to delete {count} selected custom {count, plural, =1 {entry} other {entries}}?
